name: Mounds Macaroons
recipeIngredient: 7 1/2 ounces Mounds bars, chopped into 1/2-inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
Since this is a firm dough, the macaroons will hold their shape during baking and produce large mound-shaped cookies. Preheat oven to 275°F. Line two cookie sheets with parchment paper.
1. In a medium bowl, combine flour, baking powder, and salt.
2. Using an electric mixer, beat sugar, cream cheese, and butter in a large bowl until fluffy.
3. Beat in egg, vanilla, almond extract, and orange peel.
4. Add the flour mixture and mix just until combined.
5. Stir in 12 ounces shredded sweetened coconut.
6. Add Mounds pieces and stir until combined.
7. Spread 1 1/4 cups coconut on a plate.
8. Using two tablespoons of dough for each cookie, roll dough into 1 1/2-inch rounds.
9. Roll each round in coconut.
10. Arrange the rounds 2 inches apart on prepared cookie sheets.
11. Bake until cookies are puffed and coconut is light golden, about 35 minutes.
12. Cool on cookie sheets for 5 minutes. Transfer to racks; cool completely.
13. Stir chocolate in a double boiler set over simmering water until melted and smooth. Remove from heat.
14. Dip a fork into the melted chocolate and drizzle lines of chocolate across the tops of cookies.
15. Refrigerate cookies until chocolate sets, about 30 minutes. (Can be prepared up to 3 days ahead. Refrigerate in airtight containers.)
16. Serve cold or at room temperature. Makes about 20.
